posts.hook: This 30-second grid test can detect macular degeneration before any other symptom appears.
posts.caption:
The Amsler grid.

A 10×10 grid of straight lines with a dot in the center.

How to use it:
1. Cover one eye
2. Stare at the center dot
3. Note if any lines look wavy, bent, or missing

Wavy lines = potential early macular degeneration.

AMD affects 200 million people worldwide.
It's the #1 cause of vision loss in adults over 50.

Free Amsler grid test: axeo.vision

posts.hook: Age-related macular degeneration: what it is, who gets it, and the one free test that catches it early.
posts.caption:
AMD = Age-related macular degeneration.

The macula is the center of your retina — responsible for sharp, central vision (reading, faces, driving).

AMD destroys it. Slowly. Then fast.

Two types:
Dry AMD: gradual photoreceptor loss (90% of cases)
Wet AMD: abnormal blood vessel growth — faster and more severe

Risk factors:
- Age over 50
- Smoking (doubles risk)
- Family history
- Light eyes (higher UV exposure)
- High blood pressure

The Amsler grid catches early distortion before symptoms become severe.

Free Amsler test: axeo.vision

#MacularDegeneration #AMD #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #RetinalHealth #Optometry

posts.hook: The Amsler grid test — it takes 30 seconds and could save your central vision.
posts.caption:
The Amsler grid is the only self-administered test for macular degeneration.

You don't need any equipment — just a screen.

How Axeo's Amsler test works:
1. Cover your left eye
2. Hold screen 12–14 inches away
3. Look directly at the center dot
4. Note any waviness, distortion, or missing areas
5. Repeat with right eye

Any abnormality = see an ophthalmologist immediately.

AMD affects 11 million Americans. Regular self-testing is the only way to catch changes early between eye appointments.

Free at axeo.vision — no signup required.

#AmslerGrid #MacularDegeneration #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #RetinalHealth #VisionTest

posts.hook: Why people lose their central vision — and the 5 lifestyle factors that accelerate it.
posts.caption:
Age-related macular degeneration is the leading cause of vision loss in adults over 50.

200 million people worldwide are affected.

5 factors that accelerate progression:

1. Smoking — 2× risk increase
2. UV exposure without protection
3. High glycemic diet (sugar spikes damage photoreceptors)
4. Inactivity — reduced blood flow to retina
5. Low lutein/zeaxanthin intake (these protect the macula)

The protective factors:
- Leafy greens (lutein/zeaxanthin)
- Omega-3s (DHA in retinal membranes)
- UV-blocking sunglasses
- Regular self-testing with the Amsler grid

Free test: axeo.vision

#MacularDegeneration #EyeHealth #Axeo #RetinalHealth #Lutein #VisionLoss

posts.hook: The most common cause of permanent vision loss in the developed world — and almost no one self-tests for it.
posts.caption:
AMD (age-related macular degeneration) facts:

→ #1 cause of irreversible vision loss, adults 50+
→ 200 million affected globally
→ Projected 300 million by 2040
→ Dry AMD: no treatment, only slowing
→ Wet AMD: treatable if caught early
→ Early detection window: 3–6 months before severe symptoms

The Amsler grid test takes 30 seconds.
You can do it at home.

Free at axeo.vision

posts.hook: The Amsler grid — why everyone over 40 should be checking it weekly (with free resource)
posts.caption:
r/EyeCare or r/optometry

I want to share some information about the Amsler grid because I see a lot of questions about AMD here and this doesn't come up enough.

The Amsler grid is a simple 10×10 grid with a dot in the center. You cover one eye and stare at the dot. If lines appear wavy, bent, or missing — that's a flag for macular pathology that warrants urgent ophthalmologic evaluation.

Why it matters:
- Wet AMD can progress from first symptoms to severe vision loss in weeks
- The treatment (anti-VEGF injections) is effective but only when caught early
- The treatment window is narrow — once central vision is lost, it typically doesn't return

Who should test regularly:
- Anyone over 50
- Anyone with a family history of AMD
- Anyone with known drusen (detected at eye exam)
- Smokers

Axeo has a free browser-based version at axeo.vision (no signup). Takes 30 seconds per eye.

This isn't a replacement for regular eye exams — but self-testing 2x per week catches changes between appointments, which is clinically recommended.

Happy to answer questions.
